articles.title: imd issues yellow alert as jaisalmer temperature exceeds 45°c - The News Mill
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Rajasthan
articles.full_text:
Severe heatwave conditions persist in several districts of western Rajasthan, including Jaisalmer, one of the country’s hottest desert regions. Jaisalmer and Barmer remain among the worst affected, with Jaisalmer experiencing an intense heatwave.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a yellow heatwave alert as temperatures in Jaisalmer have surpassed 45 degrees Celsius and may reach up to 47 degrees Celsius in the coming days. The extreme heat has caused mobile phones to overheat and shut down, severely affecting normal life.
During the afternoon, Jaisalmer resembled a curfew-like atmosphere, with mostly deserted roads and markets. Residents reported that although the official temperature was 46 degrees Celsius, it felt closer to 50 degrees. Attempts to avoid the oppressive heat have met with little success, and heatwave conditions are expected to continue for the next 96 hours.
Hot winds blow from 10 am until 7-8 pm, while the intense sun and rising temperatures have emptied streets and marketplaces. People have been compelled to remain indoors during peak afternoon hours.

articles.title: Severe heatwave grips Rajasthan; Jaisalmer sees curfew-like scenes - Asianet Newsable
articles.full_text:
A severe heatwave has hit western Rajasthan, with Jaisalmer among the worst-affected. Temperatures have crossed 45°C, disrupting normal life and creating a curfew-like situation. The IMD has issued a yellow alert for the region.
Severe heatwave conditions continued to grip several districts of western Rajasthan, including Jaisalmer, one of the hottest desert regions in the country. Jaisalmer and Barmer remained among the worst-affected districts, with Jaisalmer witnessing an intense heatwave. The scorching sun and relentless hot winds severely disrupted normal life, leaving residents struggling under extreme weather conditions.
Life Grinds to a Halt in Jaisalmer
With temperatures crossing 45 degrees Celsius, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued a yellow heatwave alert and warned that the mercury could rise to 47 degrees Celsius in the coming days. The intensity of the heat was evident from reports of mobile phones overheating and shutting down.
Amid the severe heat, the situation in Jaisalmer during the afternoon resembled a curfew-like atmosphere, with roads and markets remaining largely deserted. Locals said that although the recorded temperature stood at 46 degrees Celsius, it felt closer to 50 degrees.
Efforts to escape the oppressive heat have largely failed, while concerns among residents continue to grow as heatwave conditions are expected to persist over the next 96 hours. Hot winds begin blowing after 10 am and continue until 7-8 pm, while the blazing sun and rising temperatures have left streets and marketplaces nearly empty. People have been forced to remain indoors during peak afternoon hours.
Advisories Issued for Public and Livestock

articles.title: 96-Hour Heatwave Alert For Jaisalmer, Barmer As Mercury Nears 50°C - Republic World
